title: cyclobalanopsis buergeri
intro: Cyclobalanopsis buergeri, also known as the Korean chestnut or Buerger's oak, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that have been shown to have potential health benefits, such as reducing cholesterol levels and improving cardiovascular health. Traditional herbal preparations of Cyclobalanopsis buergeri include infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit cancer cell growth and improve immune function.
